skill.name: security-report
skill.description: Generate structured security assessment reports in DOCX format with findings, risk ratings, remediation guidance, and standard audit sections.
skill.summary: Create professional DOCX security assessment reports from supplied findings and risk context.
skill.author: BytR-Ecosystems

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged external command execution and weak cryptography patterns, but review found documentation examples, Markdown fences, and ordinary report-writing text. No shell execution, cryptographic implementation, network access, credential access, or prompt injection attempt was found in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: False positive: external command execution alerts
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported Ruby or shell backtick execution, but the cited areas are Python DOCX examples, Markdown code fences, workflow text, and reference guide diagrams. I found no executable shell command, Ruby backtick expression, command interpolation, or user-controlled command path.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are visible Markdown, YAML examples, or Python document-generation snippets. There is no semantic evidence of command execution or an injection vector.

low_findings.title: False positive: weak cryptographic algorithm alerts
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported weak cryptographic algorithms at lines that contain report descriptions, field labels, color guidance, and skill reference prose. I found no MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, custom hashing, password handling, or cryptographic decision logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines do not contain cryptographic primitives or security-sensitive transformations. The weak-crypto finding appears to be a pattern collision with ordinary documentation terms.

low_findings.title: Minor packaging issue: referenced advanced files are absent
low_findings.description: The skill links to advanced reference documents for executive summaries and CVSS methodology, but the reported file structure only includes SKILL.md and skills-reference.md. This affects completeness, not runtime security.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The links are present in SKILL.md, and the generated file structure lists only two files. This is a clear content completeness issue with low security impact.

content.value_statement: Security findings often need consistent reports for technical and executive readers. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to create structured DOCX reports with severity ratings and remediation plans.
actual_capabilities: Guides creation of DOCX security assessment reports.
Defines standard sections for executive summaries, scope, methodology, findings, and appendices.
Provides severity colors for Critical, High, Medium, Low, and Info findings.
Specifies detailed finding fields such as title, CVSS, evidence, remediation, and references.
Prompts severity sorting and a remediation roadmap for report planning.
limitations: Does not perform vulnerability scanning or exploit validation.
Requires the user to provide findings, evidence, and scope details.
Does not include an executable report generation script.
References advanced files that are not included in this package.

use_cases.title: Prepare Client Audit Reports
use_cases.description: Turn validated security findings into a polished DOCX report with risk ratings and remediation guidance.

use_cases.title: Summarize Internal Assessments
use_cases.description: Create consistent assessment documents for leadership, engineering, and compliance stakeholders.

use_cases.title: Document Compliance Gaps
use_cases.description: Organize control gaps, evidence, severity, and recommended actions in a structured report format.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a DOCX security assessment report from these findings. Include an executive summary, risk summary, detailed findings, and remediation roadmap.

prompt_templates.title: Format Findings by Severity
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a security report that sorts findings by Critical, High, Medium, Low, and Info. Use clear remediation steps for each finding.

prompt_templates.title: Write for Executives and Engineers
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a security assessment report with executive language in the summary and technical detail in each finding. Keep evidence and remediation specific.

prompt_templates.title: Produce a Compliance Gap Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a compliance gap assessment report. Map each gap to impact, evidence, severity, owner, remediation priority, and target completion timeline.

output_examples.input: Five web application findings with severity, evidence, and remediation notes.
output_examples.output: A DOCX assessment report with an executive summary, severity table, detailed findings, and a prioritized remediation roadmap.

output_examples.input: A compliance review with missing controls and business impact notes.
output_examples.output: A structured gap analysis document with scope, methodology, risk summary, control gaps, owners, and recommended timelines.

output_examples.input: A penetration test summary requiring leadership-ready language.
output_examples.output: A polished report that separates business impact from technical evidence and presents findings in severity order.

best_practices: Provide verified findings, clear evidence, and affected assets before generating the report.
Review severity ratings and remediation timelines before sharing the document.
Keep executive summaries concise and reserve technical detail for finding sections.
anti_patterns: Do not use the skill as a vulnerability scanner.
Do not invent evidence or CVSS values when source data is missing.
Do not publish reports before confirming scope, ownership, and remediation accuracy.

faq.question: Does this skill scan systems for vulnerabilities?
faq.answer: No. It formats supplied assessment data into a security report.

faq.question: What file format does it target?
faq.answer: It targets DOCX reports created with standard document sections and tables.

faq.question: Can it write executive summaries?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow includes a one-page executive summary section.

faq.question: Does it calculate CVSS scores automatically?
faq.answer: No. It expects CVSS data or scoring decisions to come from the user.

faq.question: Can I use it for compliance gap analysis?
faq.answer: Yes. The description lists compliance gap analysis documents as a supported use case.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found only false positive static alerts and one minor packaging completeness issue.
